snsEncryptionAtRest.^description: {$COUNT} SNS topic(s) have no server-side encryption at rest (KmsMasterKeyId is not set). Unencrypted topics store messages in plaintext at the SNS service layer, which is unacceptable for regulated workloads and blocks CloudTrail-driven audit of key usage.
snsEncryptionAtRest.shortDesc: Enable server-side encryption on every SNS topic

snsEncryptionNotCMK.^description: {$COUNT} SNS topic(s) are encrypted with the default AWS-managed key (alias/aws/sns) rather than a customer-managed KMS key. AWS-managed keys are acceptable for many workloads but do not permit key rotation control, revocation, or cross-account audit. This is an advisory finding — organisations with CMK mandates should upgrade; others can suppress.
snsEncryptionNotCMK.shortDesc: Prefer a customer-managed KMS key over alias/aws/sns

snsPublicAccess.^description: {$COUNT} SNS topic(s) have a resource policy that grants access to Principal:* without a scoping Condition (aws:SourceAccount, aws:SourceArn, aws:PrincipalOrgID, or equivalent). A wildcard principal without a condition allows any AWS account to publish or subscribe, which is almost always a misconfiguration.
snsPublicAccess.shortDesc: Constrain wildcard Principal statements with a Condition

snsNoHttpsEnforcement.^description: {$COUNT} SNS topic(s) have no policy statement that denies non-TLS traffic (no Deny + aws:SecureTransport=false). Without this, publishers can send messages over HTTP and subscribers can be delivered over HTTP, leaving in-transit traffic unencrypted.
snsNoHttpsEnforcement.shortDesc: Add a Deny statement with aws:SecureTransport=false

snsInsecureSubscription.^description: {$COUNT} SNS topic(s) have at least one subscription that uses the plain HTTP protocol (protocol=http). Messages delivered over HTTP are transmitted unencrypted. Note: email, email-json, and sms protocols are excluded from this check because they do not have TLS equivalents at the SNS layer.

snsSignatureVersionOld.^description: {$COUNT} SNS topic(s) still use SignatureVersion=1 (SHA-1) for message signing. SHA-1 is cryptographically deprecated; SignatureVersion=2 (SHA-256) is the current recommended value.

snsSubscriptionNoDlq.^description: {$COUNT} SNS topic(s) have at least one sqs/lambda/firehose subscription without a RedrivePolicy (DLQ). Without a DLQ, messages that repeatedly fail delivery are dropped silently after the retry policy is exhausted. email/sms/http(s) subscriptions are excluded because they do not support DLQs.
snsSubscriptionNoDlq.shortDesc: Configure a dead-letter queue on every async subscription

snsPendingSubscription.^description: {$COUNT} SNS topic(s) have at least one subscription in PendingConfirmation state. Pending subscriptions never receive messages until the owner confirms them; long-lived pending subscriptions usually indicate misconfigured or abandoned endpoints.

snsDeliveryStatusLoggingDisabled.^description: {$COUNT} SNS topic(s) have no delivery-status logging configured for any protocol (no *SuccessFeedbackRoleArn or *FailureFeedbackRoleArn attribute set). Without delivery-status logging, there is no CloudWatch record of which messages were successfully delivered vs. dropped, which blocks debugging and audit.

snsTracingDisabled.^description: {$COUNT} SNS topic(s) have X-Ray tracing disabled (TracingConfig is not 'Active'). Without SNS tracing, cross-service traces stop at the publisher and cannot be correlated with downstream consumers.

snsUnusedTopic.^description: {$COUNT} SNS topic(s) have zero confirmed subscriptions (SubscriptionsConfirmed=0). Topics without subscribers publish messages that go nowhere; long-idle topics are typically orphaned deployments.
snsUnusedTopic.shortDesc: Delete or subscribe to unused topics

snsResourcesWithoutTags.^description: {$COUNT} SNS topic(s) have no tags applied. Tags enable cost allocation, tag-based IAM, and lifecycle governance.
snsResourcesWithoutTags.shortDesc: Tag every SNS topic (owner, environment, cost-centre)
